Robert Silverman: Behavioral Health Counseling Consulting, Champaign, Illinois: counseling adults, teens, families groups; 2000-present.
University Illinois Counseling Center, Champaign, Illinois, Academic 2013-2014.
Military Family Life Counselor (MHNGS Magellan): Worked with military personnel their families installations around world.
Branches worked with: Army, Force, Marines, National Guard Reserves.
Part-time 2005-2009; full-time 2009-2012.
Champaign County Operation Snowball, Executive Director: 2003-2008.
Emotional intelligence leadership skills high-school-age teens.
Pavilion, psychiatric hospital.
Counselor adults teens psychiatry chemical dependency.
Champaign-Urbana Jewish Federation: Executive Director, 1995-1998.
Children's Home Society.
Prairie Center Health Systems: counseling teens, adults families regarding chemical dependency.
Robert Silverman graduated from Centennial High School Champaign, Illinois, 1969.
earned bachelor's degree Product Design, emphasizing equipment disabled children, master's degree Rehabilitation Counseling, both from Southern Illinois University, Carbondale.
later earned Master Fine Art's degree (M.F.A.)
Creative Writing, Bowling Green State University, Ohio, 1987.
Illinois Licensed Clinical Professional Counselor (LCPC) Certified Rehabilitation Counselor (CRC), Silverman worked over thirty years field behavioral health.
Silverman completed graduate school internship winter semester 1979-80 with psychiatrist Raymond Robertson, M.D., Westmont, Illinois.
Robertson (1922-2007) served Director Institute Juvenile Research Chicago, special assistant Director Illinois Department Mental Health, then medical staff Riveredge Hospital Forest Park Hinsdale Hospitals.
clinical director Riveredge Adolescent Unit from 1979 1990 time when intensive therapeutic work still done with individuals families.
liked called) also Gestalt therapist (Gestalt2) with private practice assisting clients develop what psychologist Abraham Maslow termed "Self-Actualization".
Robertson master therapist.
Having been student Frederick (Fritz) Perls (Perls2) utilized self-awareness methods Gestalt therapy only with individual clients, especially multiple, weekly therapy groups group therapeutic weekends.
After completing graduate school internship with Robertson, Silverman received further training self-awareness methods Gestalt Therapy today uses these, along with therapeutic methods, counseling practice.
Silverman earned Master Fine Art's degree (M.F.A.)
thirteen artists first year Arad Arts Project, Israel, 1992-93.
1995, trained became interviewer oral history project: Shoah Foundation's Visual History Archive.
This nonprofit organization established 1994 collect preserve testimonies Holocaust survivors.
interviewed survivors Central Southern Illinois Indiana speaker Holocaust Education committee Champaign-Urbana Jewish Federation.
Another important Holocaust education project with which involvement Wings Witness, sculpture developed York artist Jeffrey Schrier, utilizing 11.5 million tabs collected students Mahomet-Seymour Middle school, Mahomet, Illinois.
2005, Silverman recruited become Military Family Life Consultant (MFLC).
first assignment "Purple Camp," children teens whose parents been deployed.
During next four years worked many weekends with National Guard Reserve units across Illinois, well other Midwestern states their pre-deployment reintegration events, marriage enrichment retreats, Yellow Ribbon events.
2009, closed counseling practice and, until October 2012, worked numerous 60-90 assignments multiple Army, Force Marine installations around world.
These assignments were Germany, Belgium, Mainland Japan, Okinawa Japan, Greenland, England, Turkey, well Virginia, North Dakota, South Dakota, Kansas, Hawaii Alaska.
MFLC program currently managed MHNGS Magellan.
Behavioral health counseling about development integration self-awareness.
With professional degrees counseling, creative writing design, Robert Silverman integrates multiple therapeutic modalities creative self-expression skills into counseling practice.
